article_title: HBM4 Enters Mass Production as All Three Memory Giants Race to Supply NVIDIA's Vera Rubin

content_preview.title: HBM4 Enters Mass Production as All Three Memory Giants Race to Supply NVIDIA's Vera Rubin
content_preview.summary: Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron have all confirmed high-volume HBM4 production for NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform at GTC 2026, with 12-layer stacks delivering 2.8 TB/s bandwidth and 16-layer samples already shipping to customers.
content_preview.body_excerpt: The three companies that control nearly all of the world's high-bandwidth memory supply have each confirmed mass production of HBM4, the fourth generation of the stacked DRAM technology that has become the defining bottleneck of the AI hardware era. [Samsung](https://www.tomshardware.com/pc-components/gpus/nvidia-launches-vera-rubin-nvl72-ai-supercomputer-at-ces-promises-up-to-5x-greater-inference-performance-and-10x-lower-cost-per-token-than-blackwell-coming-2h-2026), [SK Hynix](https://www.tom...

recommendations: 2 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Technically detailed news article covering the HBM4 production race with clear comparative analysis of Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron strategies.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources manually verified. Micron GlobeNewswire confirmed HBM4 36GB 12-layer high-volume production, >2.8 TB/s bandwidth, >11 Gb/s pin speed, 2.3x bandwidth improvement, 20% power efficiency gain, 48GB 16-layer samples, PCIe 6.0 SSD (Micron 9650), SOCAMM2, Sumit Sadana quote. Tom's Hardware (Vera Rubin) confirmed R100 GPU with 8 HBM4 stacks, 288 GB memory, 22 TB/s bandwidth, TSMC 3nm. Tom's Hardware (Micron) confirmed Samsung February 2026 HBM4 shipments. Marvell BusinessWire confirmed CXL switch context.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key specs verified: 12-layer 36GB, >2.8 TB/s, 8 stacks per R100, 288 GB total, 22 TB/s aggregate. Samsung February 2026 shipment timeline confirmed. Market share figures (SK Hynix ~53% Q3 2025) are from industry analyst reporting. The 16-layer wafer thickness (~30 micrometers) and JEDEC 775-micrometer limit are standard industry specifications.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Technical and neutral. Appropriately covers the competitive dynamics without favoring any supplier.
editor_notes.originality: Strong comparative analysis of three suppliers' architectural approaches (TSMC vs Samsung foundry for base die).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-verified semiconductor industry news with precise technical details confirmed against primary sources. Approved.
